description: Lock Contract Law to Bronze Age Mesopotamian written-contract law, not a Classical placeholder or coined-currency-dependent doctrine.

checks.reason: The scoped claim is anchored to Sumerian/Old Babylonian law-code and written-contract practice rather than the old Classical default placeholder.
Ancient written law-code traditions supply shared legal categories, but contract practice was also documented in records and custom rather than derived from one code alone.
Cuneiform contracts, receipts, sealed documents, and accounts operationalized obligations, but the legal doctrine should not be reduced to recordkeeping.
Bronze Age contract-law evidence predates standardized coinage and used written accounts plus silver or commodity valuation.
